
### a data hazard  {.smaller}

 <!-- \lstset{
    style=small,
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<2|handout:0>]{@2@}{@},
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<3|handout:0>]{@3@}{@},
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<4|handout:0>]{@4@}{@},
    moredelim=**[is][\btHL<5|handout:0>]{@5@}{@},
} -->

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```gnuassembler
addq %r8, %r9   // R8 + R9 -> R9
addq %r9, %r8   // R9 + R8 -> R9
addq ...
addq ...
```

::::

:::: {.column}

![](/pipeline/texfig/pipelinedCpu.figure.svg){width=100%}

::::

:::::

::: {.r-stack}

![](/pipeline/texfig/addqDataHazardTable.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-out fragment-index=2 width=800px}

![](/pipeline/texfig/addqDataHazardTable.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2 width=800px}

:::

### data hazard 

```gnuassembler
addq %r8, %r9  // (1)
addq %r9, %r8  // (2)
```

<table>
<tr><th>step#</th><th>pipeline implementation</th><th>ISA specification</th></tr>
<tr><td>1    </td><td>read r8, r9 for (1)</td><td>read r8, r9 for (1) </td></tr>
<tr><td>2    </td><td>read r9, r8 for (2)</td><td>write r9 for (1) </td></tr>
<tr><td>3    </td><td>write r9 for (1)</td><td>read r9, r8 for (2) </td></tr>
<tr><td>4    </td><td>write r8 for (2)</td><td>write r8 ror (2) </td></tr>
</table> 

* pipeline reads <em>older value</em>…
* instead of value ISA says was just written
